pi_items.source: Confirmed in task.json, setup_task.sh, and export_result.sh scripts; QBlade v0.96.2 .wpa format verified from multiple sources

pi_items.verified_value: NACA 0012 - symmetric airfoil with 0% camber and 12% maximum thickness
pi_items.source: NASA NACA airfoil database (https://turbmodels.larc.nasa.gov/naca0012_val.html), UIUC Airfoil Database, Wikipedia NACA airfoil, airfoiltools.com

pi_items.verified_value: NACA 4412 - cambered airfoil with 4% camber at 40% chord and 12% maximum thickness
pi_items.source: NASA NACA database (https://turbmodels.larc.nasa.gov/naca4412sep_val.html), airfoiltools.com, aerodynamic analysis literature

Verified as critical Reynolds number where laminar boundary layer transitions to turbulent flow for smooth surfaces. Confirmed in QBlade documentation (https://docs.qblade.org/src/user/airfoil/airfoil_analysis.html), XFoil capabilities, and aerodynamic analysis literature. NASA NACA airfoil design documentation confirms this as a standard operating point.

Unable to find specific documentation confirming exact minimum file size. However, export_result.sh verifier checks for file_size > 2000 bytes as threshold for non-empty QBlade project. 5000 bytes is reasonable for project with 2 airfoils and XFoil analysis data.

privileged_info_summary: The compare_airfoil_polars task uses real aerodynamic data and established simulation tools. NACA 0012 and NACA 4412 are genuine, well-documented airfoil profiles from the NASA NACA database used extensively in aircraft and wind turbine design. Reynolds number 500,000 is a real, critical value where laminar flow transitions to turbulent flow and is a standard operating point in aerodynamic analysis. QBlade v0.96 is a legitimate open-source wind turbine design tool that integrates XFoil for airfoil analysis. The task expects the agent to generate these airfoils in QBlade and conduct XFoil direct analysis at the specified Reynolds number, saving results to a standard .wpa project file format.
